title: englerodendron mengei
intro: Englerodendron mengei, also known as the "Kadupul", is a rare and unique flowering plant native to Sri Lanka and parts of India, characterized by its ability to bloom at night and wilt by dawn. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Englerodendron mengei include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
